Steroids can help reduce inflammation and manage hypercalcemia associated with vitamin D toxicity, but they are generally not considered the first-line therapy. The primary approach involves stopping vitamin D intake and managing electrolyte imbalances through hydration and, if necessary, other specific medications like bisphosphonates.
